ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn term for a rotisserie chicken grain bowl meal with cookie. FOODON_00002473 (chicken dish) used as closest parent.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKN (chicken meat, cooked) used as closest parent for the protein component. No composite meal code exists for this specific bowl-and-cookie combination.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT food concept found for this composite fast-casual meal.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard meal serving consisting of an energy bowl with rotisserie chicken (approximately 400 g) and one cookie (approximately 150 g), totalling roughly 550 g.

nutrient_source.title: Composite estimate: USDA FoodData Central — Rotisserie Chicken (NDB 05592), Grain Bowl components (Foundation Foods), and Chocolate Chip Cookie (SR Legacy 18158)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for this composite meal exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA Branded Foods. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted-average composition: rotisserie chicken (~45% by weight, USDA FDC NDB 05592), grain/vegetable bowl base (~40% by weight, USDA Foundation Foods grain bowl components), and one chocolate chip cookie (~15% by weight, USDA SR Legacy 18158). Per-100g values derived from this weighted composite. Values represent a plausible central estimate; actual restaurant preparation may vary.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — mixed grain bowl / chicken rice composite analogue
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific Subway Energy Bowl meal. GI estimated at 52 (low category) based on analogous mixed grain-and-protein bowl entries in the University of Sydney GI Database. Mixed grain bowls with lean protein and vegetables typically fall in the GI range 45–58. The cookie component raises the composite GI slightly above a plain grain bowl. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (52 × 94.6 g carbohydrate in 550 g serving at 17.2 g/100g) / 100 ≈ 16.2.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content approximately 310 mg per 100 g; a full 550 g meal delivers approximately 1705 mg sodium
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should be aware that a single serving of this meal may provide over 70% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg; requesting reduced-sodium dressing and omitting the cookie can meaningfully lower sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index (~52) and glycemic load (~16 per meal); cookie component contributes refined carbohydrates and added sugar
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes can include this meal in a structured eating plan given its moderate GI and high protein content, but should consider omitting the cookie to reduce the glycemic load and added sugar contribution by approximately 30%.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from rotisserie chicken skin and cookie (~1.9 g/100g; ~10.5 g per meal) and elevated sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For cardiovascular disease management, this meal should be consumed without the cookie and with skin-free chicken to reduce saturated fat and sodium; the lean chicken protein and vegetable components are otherwise consistent with a heart-healthy dietary pattern.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Energy Bowl with Rotisserie Chicken and Cookie Meal is a Western fast-casual composite meal sold in Singapore, notably at Subway outlets and mix-and-match food hall counters. The meal consists of a grain-and-vegetable energy bowl topped with sliced or pulled rotisserie-roasted chicken, served alongside a baked cookie as a dessert component. The energy bowl base typically includes a grain such as rice or quinoa, mixed salad greens, and assorted vegetables, dressed with a light vinaigrette or sauce. The rotisserie chicken is slow-roasted, yielding tender, mildly seasoned meat with a slightly smoky flavour. The cookie adds sweetness and a soft, chewy texture contrast. Nutritionally, the meal is a moderate-calorie, high-protein option (approximately 175 kcal and 12.5 g protein per 100 g) with a moderate glycemic index of approximately 52. The sodium content is elevated at approximately 310 mg per 100 g, making portion awareness important for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The meal is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those with gluten or dairy allergies. It is commonly ordered as a lunch or dinner option and is priced at approximately SGD 15.20 on field survey data.

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry for this specific composite meal exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or any primary regional database. Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted composite of USDA FoodData Central entries for rotisserie chicken, grain bowl components, and a chocolate chip cookie. The GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component-level USDA sources are high-quality approved sources and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates derived from USDA FoodData Central component entries (rotisserie chicken NDB 05592, grain bowl base Foundation Foods, chocolate chip cookie SR Legacy 18158) weighted by approximate serving proportions (45% chicken, 40% grain-vegetable bowl, 15% cookie). Actual restaurant preparation will vary. 2. Vitamin D is -1 as it was not reported in the USDA FoodData Central component entries used for this composite estimate. 3. Vitamin E is -1 as it was not reported in the USDA FoodData Central component entries used for this composite estimate. 4. Selenium is -1 as it was not reported in the USDA FoodData Central component entries used for this composite estimate. 5. GI of 52 is an analogous estimate based on mixed grain-and-protein bowl entries in the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for this specific product exists. 6. Halal status is listed as false because Subway Singapore's halal certification status for all outlets cannot be confirmed universally; individual outlet certification should be verified. 7. The food_name has been normalised from the input 'Energy Bowl Rotisserie Chicken Cookie Meal' to 'Energy Bowl with Rotisserie Chicken and Cookie (Meal)' for canonical clarity. 8. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, and water_g are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data and carry medium confidence.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Drafted full composite nutrient profile from USDA FoodData Central component entries. Added all mandatory multilingual names for Singaporean cuisine context. Confirmed no zero core macros requiring justification.

For g3_numeric_hygiene, refined the documentation for -1 values (vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, selenium_mcg) in the 'notes' field to explicitly mention 'USDA FoodData Central component entries' as the source where data was not reported. For q8_aliases_and_multilingual_synonym_coverage, added the canonical 'food_name' ('Energy Bowl with Rotisserie Chicken and Cookie (Meal)') to both the flat 'aliases' list and the 'aliases_by_language.english' array to ensure complete consistency and comprehensiveness.
